checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the text found in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified. All headings and table entries correspond to the source text.
No contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses, URLs) is present in the source document or the output.
All sections and both pages from the source PDF are represented in the output JSON. Page 1 (Goal 1 and its sub-goals) and Page 2 (Goal 2 and its sub-goals) are fully accounted for.
The row counts in the output tables match the source document exactly. Table 1 (Goal 1) has 15 rows in both source and output. Table 2 (Goal 2) has 8 rows in both source and output.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output captures the full text content of each section. While some multiline entries in the source table are condensed into single JSON string values (e.g., in sub-goal 1.7 or 2.3), the complete textual information is preserved.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Minor differences observed (such as character normalization of hyphens/dashes and whitespace within cells) do not alter the meaning of the content and fall within acceptable reformatting limits for document conversion.

checks.a_analysis: The content appears unique and follows the source document without unintended repetitions. Blocks like 'SGUC – identify common opportunities and requirements' and 'LGC – identify common opportunities and requirements' appearing together in Table 1 Row 15 accurately reflect the distinct entries in the source.

The document title is correctly identified as H1. Major goal sections (Goal 1, Goal 2) are identified as paragraphs, which is an acceptable normalization for screen reader navigation in this context. The sub-goal identifiers (1.1, 2.1, etc.) are embedded within tables, which preserves their logical relationship to the data rows.

The table structure is corrupted in terms of column consistency and header identification. In both Table 1 and Table 2, rows containing sub-goal titles (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1) consist of a single cell with 'colspan: 1', whereas the content rows immediately below them contain 2 cells. This creates a jagged table structure where headers do not span the full width of the table. Additionally, the identification of these sub-goal rows is inconsistent; for instance, in Table 1, Goal 1.1 is marked as a 'header' row while Goal 1.2 is marked as 'data'.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document shows Goal 1.1 through 1.7 and 2.1 through 2.4 as header rows spanning the full width of the 2-column data grid below them.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document, correctly sequencing the goals and their corresponding data tables.

The document does not contain explicit structural lists; multi-line items within table cells (like those in Goal 1.7) are represented as text with newlines, which accurately reflects the source's visual layout without bullets.
No links are present in the source document, and none have been erroneously added to the JSON output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. Both documents are structured around two primary goals, each followed by a set of numbered objectives presented in a table-like format. The transition from the Goal headings to the sub-goals and their respective data points is logically followed in the HTML. Although the font and background colors differ (e.g., loss of blue shading in headers), the visual flow and grouping of information are consistent with the source document.

The tables in the HTML are well-rendered. Columns are aligned, and borders are visible, allowing a user to clearly distinguish between rows and columns. The sub-goal headers (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1) are visually distinct from the content rows by spanning the full width of the table and using bold text. For more complex items like 1.7, the multi-line content in the right column is correctly grouped within its cell, ensuring the data remains understandable.
The source PDF is a text-and-table based document with no logos, charts, photos, or other meaningful images. Therefore, no images are missing from the HTML output.

There is no evidence of body content being duplicated in the HTML rendering. Each goal, sub-goal, and data point appears exactly once in the document flow, matching the structure of the two-page source PDF.
